memoryArtifacts.headline: Henry III of Glogów (Polish: Henryk; 1251/60 - 11 December 1310)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Henry III of Glogów (Polish: Henryk; 1251/60 - 11 December 1310) was a duke of Glogów from 1274 to his death and also duke of parts of Greater Poland during 1306 - 1310.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Heinrich III Duke of Silesia-Glogau於1257年出生時，他的父親Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów29歲，母親Salome of Greater Poland27歲。他和Mechthild von Braunschweig-Lüneburg至少有5個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1309年12月9日在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇傑史萊先享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: Duke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English duk(ke), duck, doke, dook ‘duck’ (Old English dūce), either from a perceived resemblance (perhaps a waddling gait) or from association with wild fowling. Compare Duck , Drake .

English: from the Middle English personal name Duk or Duke. In northern England this is usually a pet form of Marmaduke. It may alternatively be a survival of one or more Old English personal names, though it is uncertain whether they were still current in the period of surname formation. Old English Ducc(a) is attested in placenames like Duxford (Cambridgeshire) and Duckington (Cheshire), and was perhaps interchangeable with Docc, attested in Doxey (Staffordshire) and Doxford (Northumberland). Duke could also represent Old English Deowuc (as in Deuxhill, Shropshire). A surname from Marmaduke is on record until at least 1881 and derives from the personal name Marmaduke, apparently an Anglo-Norman French pronunciation of Old Irish Maolmaedóc ‘devotee of Maedóc’; see Duckett .
Americanized form of Polish Duk: nickname from dukać ‘to stammer or falter’.

variants.name: Dock
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Duke  .
Norwegian: habitational name from a farm named Dokk, from Old Norse dǫkk ‘hollow, depression’.
North German (Lower Saxony): possibly a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked in the cloth trade, from Middle Low German dōk ‘fabric’.

variants.name: Doke
meanings: English: variant of   Duke  .

variants.name: Duch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Nga, Phal, Phan, Sophan, Tha, Thang, Yen.  German Kurt, Klaus.
Polish, Sorbian, and Ukrainian: nickname from duch ‘spirit, breath’.
Czech: from a short form of the Old Czech personal name Duchoslav, composed of the elements duch ‘spirit’ + slav ‘glory’ or ‘famous’.

variants.name: Duckett
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Duket. This may sometimes have been an early Middle English pet form of an Old English Ducc(a), but was more often a pet form of Marmaduke, especially in Yorkshire (see Duke ). Marmaduke itself is from Irish Maol Maedóc ‘servant of Maedóc’, Maedóc being the name of prominent Irish saints of the 6th and 7th centuries.

variants.name: Duck
meanings: English: variant of   Duke  .
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Lohan (an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Leocháin ‘descendant of Leochán’) by mistranslation, as if from lacha ‘duck’.
North German (also Dück) and German (Dück): see Dueck .

variants.name: Dukes
meanings: English: variant of Duke , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Heinrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from heim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power’. See Henry .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Heinrich III Duke of Silesia-Glogau was born in 1257, his father, Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów, was 29 and his mother, Salome of Greater Poland, was 27. He had at least 5 sons and 3 daughters with Mechthild von Braunschweig-Lüneburg. He died on 9 December 1309, in Silesia, Bohemia, Holy Roman Empire, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heinrich III Duke of Silesia-Glogau est né en 1257, son père, Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Salome of Greater Poland, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles avec Mechthild von Braunschweig-Lüneburg. Il est décédé le 9 décembre 1309, à Schlesien, Bohême, Saint-Empire romain, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heinrich III Duke of Silesia-Glogau 1257 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów 29 und seine Mutter Salome of Greater Poland 27. Er hatte mit Mechthild von Braunschweig-Lüneburg mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 9. Dezember 1309 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Schlesien, Böhmen, Heiliges Römisches Reich.

Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie con Mechthild von Braunschweig-Lüneburg. Morì il 9 dicembre 1309, nel Schlesien, Čechy, Heiliges Römisches Reich, all'età di 52 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Heinrich III Duke of Silesia-Glogau(이)가 1257년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Salome of Greater Poland(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Mechthild von Braunschweig-Lüneburg(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1309년 12월 9일 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 cc히 슐레시에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas com Mechthild von Braunschweig-Lüneburg. Ele faleceu em 9 de dezembro de 1309, em Schlesien, Čechy, Heiliges Römisches Reich, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heinrich III Duke of Silesia-Glogau родился в 1257 г., его отцу по имени Konrad I of Glogów Duke of Glogów было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Salome of Greater Poland, 27. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери от Mechthild von Braunschweig-Lüneburg. Он умер 9 декабря 1309 г. в местности щлесиен, cечи, хилигесь рoмищесь раич в возрасте 52 лет.
